title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Gwendolyn reflects on the murder of Lily Kimball, suspecting that either Walter Fane or Jackie Afflick tampered with the brandy and used a prank phone call to implicate the other. Gwendolyn considers Walter Fane, a quiet man who may have been driven to violence by past humiliations involving a woman named Helen. She then shifts her suspicion to Afflick, theorizing that his arrogant demeanor masks a cruel nature and an inferiority complex. She believes Afflick killed Lily because she threatened him and now intends to kill Gwendolyn and Jervis to protect his secrets, potentially framing Walter Fane in the process.

While contemplating whether Richard Erskine’s wife might be involved due to a suspicious man's voice on the phone, Gwendolyn is interrupted by Dr. Kennedy entering the house. In a moment of intense psychological terror, Gwendolyn hallucinates or perceives Dr. Kennedy as the killer, accusing him of murdering Helen and Lily. He approaches her, claiming that her interference has brought back the memory of Helen and that he must kill Gwendolyn just as he killed Helen. As he reaches for her throat, Gwendolyn is paralyzed by fear in the empty house. The tension is broken when Miss Marple runs up the stairs and accidentally sprays soap water into Dr. Kennedy's eyes while attempting to spray aphids on his roses.
